verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is canonically a cytosolic chaperone lacking a TM domain or secretory signal. However, multiple independent studies using flow cytometry on viable cells, surface biotinylation, and cell-surface iodination confirm that HSPA1A translocates to and embeds in the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane specifically in tumor cells and heat-stressed cells, driven by high-affinity binding to saturated phosphatidylserine and Gb3 in lipid rafts. Normal resting cells do not display surface HSPA1A. The C-terminal domain is exposed extracellularly on viable tumor cells and is targeted by clinical-grade mAb cmHsp70.1. This is a well-documented oncogenic/stress-state ecto-pool qualifying as cell_state_induced.
